original-actions: Recognizes Siegfried immediately upon his arrival at the Burgundian court in Worms
Recounts Siegfried's legendary deeds to King Gunther and the assembled court
Advises Gunther against wooing Brunhild, warning of the mortal danger
Accompanies the expedition to Iceland to win Brunhild for Gunther
Assists Gunther during Brunhild's three strength contests
Swears vengeance against Siegfried to restore Brunhild's honor after her humiliation
Questions Kriemhild about Siegfried's vulnerability under pretense of protecting him
Tricks Kriemhild into revealing the one spot where Siegfried can be wounded
Persuades Kriemhild to sew a cross on Siegfried's garment marking the vulnerable spot
Fabricates a false report of a Saxon invasion to justify a hunting trip
Organizes the hunting expedition to create an opportunity for murder
Hides Siegfried's sword and weapons before the attack
Hurls a spear through Siegfried's back as he drinks from a stream
Endures Siegfried's dying counterattack with his shield
Places Siegfried's corpse on Kriemhild's threshold before dawn
Spreads a false account claiming robbers killed Siegfried
Approaches Siegfried's bier, causing the wounds to bleed afresh and proving his guilt
Urges the Burgundian kings to seize Kriemhild's Nibelung treasure
Sinks the treasure in the Rhine to prevent Kriemhild from raising an army
Warns King Gunther against accepting Kriemhild's invitation to Etzel's court
Predicts that the journey is a trap for revenge
Yields to Giselher's taunts and agrees to make the journey
Encounters water sprites bathing at the Danube crossing
Steals the sprites' garments to compel them to prophesy
Receives the prophecy that only the chaplain will return from Hunland
Tricks the ferryman by claiming to be Amelrich, Else's liegeman
Kills the ferryman with his sword after the ferryman attacks him
Seizes the ferry and pilots the Burgundian army across the Danube
Throws the royal chaplain overboard to test the prophecy
Destroys the ferry after crossing to eliminate any chance of retreat
Leads the defense against Margrave Gelfrat's Bavarian forces in retaliation
Defeats the Bavarians, losing only four men against over a hundred slain
Arrives at Etzel's court openly wearing Siegfried's sword Balmung
Refuses to rise when Kriemhild approaches, defying courtly custom
Admits openly to killing Siegfried when Kriemhild demands to know the murderer
Repels Kriemhild's vassals through intimidation alongside Volker
Stands guard with Volker through the night, refusing to sleep
Kills a Hun at the night-watch who attempted to attack them
Beheads young Prince Ortlieb at the feast, triggering the final massacre
Fights in the great hall battle alongside the Burgundian warriors
Slays the warrior Iring in single combat
Survives the burning of the hall by sheltering behind shields and drinking blood
Refuses to reveal the location of the Nibelung treasure to Kriemhild
Convinces Kriemhild to kill Gunther first, promising to reveal the hoard's location
Refuses to reveal the treasure after Gunther's death, declaring she will never learn it
Dies by Kriemhild's hand, slain with Siegfried's own sword Balmung
generalized-actions: Recognizes renowned hero immediately upon arrival at court
Recounts hero's legendary deeds to king and assembled court
Advises king against dangerous courtship, warning of mortal peril
Accompanies expedition to foreign realm to win bride for king
Assists king during suitor's strength contests
Swears vengeance against hero to restore queen's honor
Questions confidant about hero's vulnerability under pretense of protection
Tricks confidant into revealing hero's single vulnerable spot
Persuades confidant to mark hero's vulnerable spot on garment
Fabricates false invasion report to justify hunting expedition
Organizes outing to create opportunity for assassination
Hides victim's weapons before the attack
Strikes victim through back at moment of vulnerability
Endures victim's dying counterattack behind shield
Places victim's corpse at widow's threshold before dawn
Spreads false account blaming murder on robbers
Approaches victim's bier, causing wounds to bleed and reveal guilt
Urges kings to seize widow's legendary treasure
Sinks treasure in river to prevent widow raising army
Warns king against accepting enemy's invitation to foreign court
Predicts journey is trap for revenge
Yields to companion's taunts and agrees to make the journey
Encounters water spirits at river crossing
Steals spirits' garments to compel prophecy
Receives prophecy that only one will return alive
Tricks ferryman by claiming false identity
Kills ferryman after ferryman attacks
Seizes ferry and pilots army across river
Throws holy man overboard to test prophecy
Destroys crossing after passage to eliminate retreat
Leads defense against pursuing forces in retaliation
Defeats pursuing army with minimal losses against heavy casualties
Arrives at enemy court openly wearing victim's legendary sword
Refuses to rise for enemy, defying courtly custom
Admits openly to murder when enemy demands confession
Repels enemy's vassals through intimidation alongside companion
Stands night watch with companion, refusing to sleep
Kills attacker during night watch
Beheads young heir at feast, triggering massacre
Fights in great hall battle alongside companions
Slays enemy champion in single combat
Survives burning hall behind shields, drinking blood to endure
Refuses to reveal treasure location to enemy
Convinces enemy to kill liege first, falsely promising secret
Refuses to reveal secret after liege's death, defiant to end
Dies by enemy's hand, slain with victim's own blade
